_meta.purpose: Authoritative live state for JobTrackr-CV. Read at session start. Update on significant changes.

project.name: JobTrackr-CV
project.summary: AI-assisted job discovery + CV tailoring SaaS. One codebase, one Supabase DB, three services: frontend/web (Vercel), backend/worker (Fly), backend/api (Fly).

web.stack: Next.js 16 (Turbopack) App Router, Tailwind v4, Supabase browser client
web.deploy: git push origin main — auto-deploys to production

worker.notes: Stage-4b filter is title-only + optional teaser rescue (driven by profile.must_include_phrases, migration 046). Primary SEEK: seekDirect (curl_cffi Chrome-124 TLS, no proxy); Apify actor: fallback only via profile.seek_method='actor'. Careerjet: direct HTML scraping via curl_cffi (API abandoned to avoid tracking URL IP-blocks). Adzuna: API teasers by default (profile.adzuna_method='api'); opt-in 'direct' mode scrapes /details/<id> HTML for full JDs (migration 047, cap 50). Full-JD threshold: 1400 chars. Jora: disabled (OOM on 512MB VM).

cv_backend.stack: FastAPI, Python 3.11, ReportLab, Supabase service-role client

build_state.billing_high_level: Adding paid subscriptions (Stripe, AUD) gating the platform. AI cost is now the platform's (BYOK removed 2026-06-16, see D20) — plan caps/pricing below were set when AI cost sat with the user and need revisiting. Plans: 3-day card-upfront TRIAL (3 tailored CVs + 3 cover letters, 1 profile, 1 run) → WEEKLY (50 unique / 75 total CVs + letters, 5 profiles, 30 runs) → MONTHLY (250 unique / 375 total, 10 profiles, 120 runs) → UNLIMITED (∞, higher price). Total = unique × 1.5. Tailored CV and cover letter are SEPARATE buckets, equal caps (either/or). Caps reset per Stripe billing period (counted by current_period_start). Auto-renew ON (Stripe default; trial auto-converts after 3 days; subscriptions auto-renew each period). On cancel/expiry → READ-ONLY (existing CVs/letters viewable, no new analyses). Existing role='beta' users grandfathered via a comp subscription with a 14-day grace. Enforcement is at the 4 web-layer choke points only (createProfile, profiles/[id]/run, jobs/[id]/analyze, jobs/[id]/cover-letter); cv-backend + worker untouched. Migration 051_billing.sql. Pricing numbers are placeholders to finalize before go-live.

client_side_filtering.summary: Both dashboard (/dashboard) and profile board (/dashboard/profiles/[id]/jobs) use instant client-side filtering.
client_side_filtering.pattern: Server fetches all ≤200 non-dismissed jobs once, computes progress/pipelineState/atsBand. Client component holds full set and applies filterJobs+sortJobs in useMemo. Stage/triage/sort/keywords use shallowSetParams (History API) — no server round-trip. Location/posted_within/dismissed still hit server.

pipeline_automation.lifecycle: DISCOVERED → JD_CHECK → ROLE_CHECK → INITIAL_ATS_GATE(60) → TAILORED_CV → FINAL_ATS_GATE(70) → COVER_LETTER+EMAIL → APPLICATION_READY → READY_TO_SEND/APPLY → SENT/APPLIED
pipeline_automation.gates_save_cost: JD_CHECK: zero AI cost; ROLE_CHECK: zero AI cost; INITIAL_GATE: saves ~3 AI calls; FINAL_GATE: saves ~2 AI calls
pipeline_automation.cover_letter: Co-generated with email body+subject in ONE AI call. Voice profile (fingerprint + sample) drives register.

billing.summary: Stripe subscriptions (AUD) monetize the platform. Enforcement lives ONLY in the web layer at 4 choke points; backend/api and backend/worker are unaware of billing.
billing.model: Two metered buckets (tailored_cv, cover_letter) + profiles (live count of search_profiles) + runs (per-period event count). consume_usage() Postgres RPC does an atomic cap-check + reservation (FOR UPDATE on subscription). Reserve on trigger (pending) → trigger on analysis_runs/cover_letters status flips to committed (completed) or voided (failed). Dangling pending self-heals after 1h TTL in the count.
billing.entitlement_resolution: founder/admin → unlimited bypass. status='trialing' → TRIAL limits (3/3/1/1) regardless of chosen plan, window = trial period. status='active' → chosen plan limits. status='past_due' → access continues (dunning banner) until Stripe moves to canceled/unpaid. plan='comp' (grandfathered beta) → unlimited until current_period_end (14-day grace) then read-only. canceled/unpaid/no-sub → READ-ONLY + redirect to /onboarding/plan.

structured_cv_review_form.summary: Every uploaded CV passes through a normalising review form before the analysis pipeline consumes it. Removes input variance across thousands of CV layouts. Edits are rearrangement only — verbatim words preserved.
structured_cv_review_form.version: STRUCTURED_CV_VERSION = 4 (frontend/web/src/lib/cvBackend.ts + backend/api/app/services/cv/cv_structurizer.py — bump in lockstep). Review page silently re-runs structurize when stored _version is below this.
structured_cv_review_form.data_flow: Upload → /api/cv (POST) runs structurizeCv + categoriseCv in parallel → merges categoriser skills into structured_cv.skills (mirror) → renders normalized_cv_text via cv-backend /internal/render-canonical-cv → INSERT cv_versions row (cv_text, categorised_skills, structured_cv, structured_cv_status='parsed', normalized_cv_text, is_active iff first upload). Review form PATCH /api/cv/[id]/structured re-renders normalized_cv_text and keeps categorised_skills in lockstep with structured_cv.skills. /api/jobs/[id]/analyze reads normalized_cv_text when present, falls back to cv_text.
structured_cv_review_form.schema_shape: { summary, experience[], education[], awards[], languages[], certifications[], skills{technical,soft_skills,domain_knowledge}, references[], gaps[], _version }. NO contact (profile is source of truth via stamp_contact_line).
bucketing_rules: Care-sector VET quals (Cert III/IV / Diploma in Ageing Support, Individual Support, Community Services, etc.) live in education[] ONLY. Router moves any leaked-into-certifications copy and dedupes by fuzzy key that strips AU unit-code prefix (CHC43015 - X == X).

Awards / Recognition / Honours / Commendations / Achievements / Scholarships → awards[] (not certifications). Recognized by section heading OR by award pattern (X Award, X Scholarship, Recognized for...) even when PDF mashes the heading into adjacent prose.
Languages → own block (preserved but not used by tailored-CV composer).
Experience sorted reverse-chronological by parsed end_date; Present/is_current ranks highest, undated last.
structured_cv_review_form.cv_library_ux: CV cards expand inline (no route change); entire card is the click target with hover-lift; Expand/Collapse button. References card matches the same shell. Active CV is always first. Eager-loaded structured_cv makes expand instant; partial CVs still hit POST /structurize on demand. ensureSomeoneActive() runs on the page load AND on every mutate so the library never shows zero-active when CVs exist.
structured_cv_review_form.application_pool_rule: /dashboard/applications pool requires a COMPLETE set per job: cover letter (status=completed, !stale) + analysis_run (!stale) + tailored CV path (tailored_pdf_storage_path OR tailored_cv_storage_path). Sidebar badge count uses the same rule (frontend/web/src/app/(dashboard)/layout.tsx) so they cannot diverge. Sent tab unchanged (applied_at NOT NULL AND NOT dismissed).
structured_cv_review_form.tailored_cv_references_format: Plain-text lines, no markdown table. Format: '**Name**, Job title, Company — email@example.com'. Header row 'Referee | Email' and the markdown divider line were removed from build_references_block in backend/api/app/services/cv/contact_line.py.

css_theming.rule: Always use var(--brand), border-border, text-text, bg-surface. NEVER hardcode hex.
css_theming.default: Notion is the project default (getStoredTheme() + the FOUC script in app/layout.tsx fall back to 'notion' when nothing is stored; an explicit 'default' choice is still honoured).
css_theming.public_pages: Landing (app/landing.css scoped to .landing), auth (login/signup inline styles) and privacy do NOT consume theme tokens — they were hand-restyled to the Notion palette (lavender canvas #E6E0F5 / surface #F4EEFB, purple #5645D4 accent, deep purple/navy #241A5C dark sections) + Manrope/Noto Serif. Changing the Notion theme later won't auto-update these.

Dayforce CRACKED + adapter rewritten (agedCareDayforce.ts, adapter.name='agedcare_dayforce', source='agedcare', tier 2, vertical=healthcare). Was PAUSED in D23 ('403s even via curl_cffi'). Root cause (validated 2026-06-29 via DevTools cURL): NOT Cloudflare/TLS — the old request sent the WRONG body (jobBoardCode 'CANDIDATEPORTAL' + page/pageSize) and NO CSRF. Real flow = BOOTSTRAP then SEARCH: (1) GET https://jobs.dayforcehcm.com/en-AU/{namespace}/{board}?searchText= → harvest Set-Cookie (__Host-next-auth.csrf-token + __cf_bm + NamespaceCookie); x-csrf-token header = the csrf cookie value before the '|' (decodeURIComponent then split). (2) POST /api/geo/{namespace}/jobposting/search with that cookie jar + X-CSRF-Token + body {clientNamespace, jobBoardCode:{board}, cultureCode:'en-AU', searchText:'', distanceUnit:1, paginationStart}. Response {jobPostings[], maxCount, offset, count}; 25/page, paginate paginationStart+=25 until offset+count>=maxCount. Per posting: jobTitle, jobDescription (full JD inline, HTML), postingLocations[0].formattedAddress (BEST location data — full street addr+state+postcode), postingStart/ExpiryTimestampUTC. Plain Node fetch works (no curl_cffi/proxy — non-Chrome TLS passed, so Cloudflare is passive here). First tenant Uniting NSW/ACT (unitingaunsw/UNITINGCCS): 146 listed → 66 care-role full JDs, 0 thin, 21s. Job-detail URL guessed as /en-AU/{ns}/{board}/jobs/{id} (JD already captured; verify apply link). Opal HealthCare ADDED + validated 2026-06-29 (namespace opalhealthcare, board CANDIDATEPORTALNURSING — a full care portal, 87 listed → 86 role-matched). Dayforce = 2 tenants, 152 jobs. Activated in adapters[]; enablement migration 075 + VALID_SOURCES + PlatformSourcesCard ('Aged Care — Uniting'). Test: testDayforce.ts. NEEDS: flyctl deploy worker + apply migration 075 (batched with more Uniting tenants per user).

Avature adapter REWRITTEN + validated (avature.ts, adapter.name='avature', source='agedcare', vertical=healthcare, tier 3). First tenant: Regis Aged Care (regis.avature.net, 84 homes). KEY FINDING (validated 2026-06-29 via DevTools recon): Regis's Avature board server-renders the FULL JD inline in each listing card — detail pages carry NO schema.org JSON-LD (grep=0). So unlike Workday/Radancy (JSON-LD detail fetch), this adapter is LIST-ONLY: paginate ?jobRecordsPerPage=6&jobOffset=0,6,12 (page size fixed at 6; jobRecordsPerPage ignored), split on <article class='...article--result'>, lock onto the clean <h3 ...title> /JobDetail/{slug}/{id} link (each job ALSO appears 3x as twitter/linkedin/facebook share links — dedupe by job id), role-filter title via matchRole, extract inline JD from <div class='article__content'> (slice from content to card end, cut trailing shareButton UI), parse location (span.list-item-location), ref (span.list-item-ref), close-date (span.list-item-date 'Close date 31-Jul-2026' → expires_at). Cheapest aged-care adapter (no detail fetches). Cookie: portalLanguage-15=en_US suffices (no session cookie needed). LIVE TEST: 120 listed → 59 role-matched full JDs (~1.7k chars), 1 thin (a blank duplicate RN posting), 28.8s. Reusable for other Avature aged-care tenants (add to ORGS). Activated in adapters[]; enablement migration 074 (unlimited tier) + VALID_SOURCES + PlatformSourcesCard ('Aged Care — Regis'). Test: testAvature.ts. NEEDS: flyctl deploy worker + apply migration 074. NEXT (P3): Uniting is state-federated (QLD=Workday done; NSW/ACT uniting.org, Vic/Tas unitingvictas.org.au, AgeWell, SA, WA = separate orgs/ATS TBD — validate apply-link domain each).

Radancy/TalentBrew adapter added (radancy.ts, adapter.name='radancy', source='agedcare', vertical=healthcare, tier 3). Bupa AU aged care is on Radancy (careers.bupa.com.au), NOT Workday (bupa.wd3 is UK-only). Validated 2026-06-29: search-jobs page server-renders /job/{city}/{slug}/{companyId}/{jobId} links; detail pages carry clean schema.org JSON-LD JobPosting (full HTML JD + structured AU address w/ country + datePosted). Approach: paginate via /search-jobs/results AJAX (RecordsPerPage=100; JSON 'results' field holds rendered job HTML) with static /search-jobs fallback → role-taxonomy pre-filter on slug → fetch detail → JSON-LD JD → AU-country filter. Static ?p=N pagination does NOT work (returns page 1). Enabled on unlimited tier via migration 073; added to VALID_SOURCES + PlatformSourcesCard ('Aged Care — Bupa'). Test: testRadancy.ts. Reusable for other Radancy employers (add to ORGS). Also: agedCareWorkday MAX_DETAIL_FETCH removed (Infinity), MAX_PAGES 35->60. PAGINATION FIXED 2026-06-29 (commit 5ec41b6): the /search-jobs/results AJAX endpoint requires the FULL TalentBrew query string — sending only CurrentPage&RecordsPerPage=100 returns {hasJobs:true, results:''} (empty), forcing the static fallback (15 links -> 3 role matches). Root cause = missing SearchType=5 plus the full distance/sort/facet param set + Content-Type:application/json and Referer headers (captured via DevTools 'Copy as cURL'). collectLinks now builds the full query via resultsUrl() (only CurrentPage varies), RecordsPerPage=15 (100 returns degenerate), MAX_PAGES 40 safety ceiling, loop breaks early when a page yields no new /job/ links. No Imperva cookie session needed — params alone fixed it. LIVE TEST (residential IP): 269 links -> 37 role-matched -> 37 full JDs (0 thin), up from 3. Migrations applied through 073. Next priorities (P2 Workday-at-scale + Fly egress; P3 expand) in docs/aged-care-scraping-handoff.md.

SuccessFactors (SAP) CSB adapter built (successFactors.ts, adapter.name='successfactors', source='agedcare', tier 3, vertical=healthcare). PRIME target — one adapter unlocks many AU aged-care providers (Workday-style leverage). First tenant Australian Unity (careers.australianunity.com.au). Built from the documented SF Career-Site-Builder pattern, mirrors radancy.ts: GET /search/?q=&startrow=N server-rendered listing (25/page, paginate via startrow) -> collect /job/{slug}/{id} links -> role-taxonomy pre-filter on de-slugged path -> fetch detail -> schema.org JSON-LD JobPosting (CSB embeds it for SEO) -> full JD + AU-country filter. Imperva (visid_incap/incap_ses) expected passive like Bupa/Radancy (UA only, no cookie); if a SEQUENCE 202-challenges (Clinch-style AWS-WAF), needs cookie bootstrap/headless. VALIDATED 2026-06-30: Australian Unity 123 links -> 89 role-matched -> 89 full JDs, 0 thin, 37s. REALITY DIFFERED FROM PLAN: CSB detail pages here carry NO JSON-LD — JD is in <span itemprop=description class=jobdescription> (balanced-span extraction), title from <title> (strip 'Job Details | {Company}'), suburb/STATE parsed from the rich slug ({Suburb}-{Title}-{STATE}-{postcode}). JSON-LD kept as a fallback for future CSB themes that emit it. JOB_LINK_RE keeps %2C/& slugs + trailing slash (detail URL needs the trailing slash). Minor cosmetic: a slug word before the role keyword can leak into the suburb ('Kilsyth Night Duty'), real suburb still present/geocodable. Registered in adapters[] + exports. Migration 076 (unlimited tier) + VALID_SOURCES + PlatformSourcesCard ('Aged Care — Aus Unity'). Test: testSuccessFactors.ts. Worker typecheck clean. Add more SF tenants = one ORGS row after the 2-curl recon. NEEDS: flyctl deploy worker + apply migration 076.

Two pipeline bugs fixed (worker, branch claude/direct-career-site-scraping-r6vq44). (1) INVALID JOB LINKS: pipeline/normalise.ts canonicalUrl() lowercased the WHOLE href — fine for host (the URL ctor already lowercases the case-insensitive scheme+host) but it mangled case-sensitive ATS PATHS (Avature /en_US/careers/JobDetail/, SuccessFactors /job/{Slug}/) → dead links users couldn't open/copy-JD/apply. Fix: return u.href without .toLowerCase() (dedup unaffected, host still normalized). SEPARATE structural bug in agedCareWorkday.ts: emitted URL omitted the board segment (base+externalPath) → 404; fixed to base+/{board}+externalPath (e.g. /Anglicare_Careers/job/...). Workday + Dayforce (/en-AU/{ns}/{board}/jobs/{id}) links click-verified working 2026-06-30; Radancy/Avature/SF already emit the real listing href. testAgedCareWorkday/testDayforce now print sample URLs. EXISTING agedcare rows still hold the old lowercased/board-less URLs — needs `delete from jobs where source='agedcare'` + Full refresh after deploy to repopulate with valid URLs. (2) MISSING DISTANCES: aged-care job locations carry facility/provider/shift noise (Mercy Place Parkville, St Johns Village Glebe, Kilsyth Night Duty, Nowra Aboriginal) that Nominatim couldn't geocode → distance_km null → no distance sort. Fix in lib/distance.ts candidatesFor(): strip PROVIDER_PREFIX/FACILITY_WORDS/SHIFT_NOISE + try each comma segment → bare suburb resolves (tested vs real samples). PLUS orchestrator stage 11b now falls back to the profile SEARCH location as the distance origin when no home_address is set (user-chosen option 1) so distance works from just the Location field. Dashboard distance sort was never gated on home_address (stale comment) — no frontend change needed; it just needed non-null distance_km. NEEDS: flyctl deploy worker.

Work-setting classification + per-profile filter (branch feat/setting-classifier, Migration 078). PROBLEM: Cert III/IV ageing-support (and nursing broadly) is one credential across very different SETTINGS; users want to keep e.g. facility roles and drop in-home/mobile. The setting is encoded semantically in the full JD, not the title — so hard keyword rules resolve the confident majority and an optional AI call adjudicates the ambiguous middle. TAXONOMY (4): hospital_clinical, residential_aged_care (retirement village folded in), home_community (in-home + travel-between-clients merged per user), other/unclear; NULL for non-care jobs. TWO OPERATIONS kept separate: (a) CLASSIFICATION is a shared, once-per-job FACT (like sponsorship_status) computed at worker stage 10c and stored on global_jobs, reused by every profile; (b) FILTER is per-profile, applied at serve time (bucket) / stage 10d (legacy) — NEVER before the shared bucket write, else it would poison discovery for other profiles. FAIL-OPEN: only drop a CONFIDENT (>=0.7) non-matching category; NULL/other/low-confidence always surfaced. COST: care-vertical gate + keyword-first; AI fallback off by default (env SETTING_CLASSIFIER_AI), so ships rules-only and we measure the ambiguous rate first. Manual pastes (thin JD via PATCH /api/jobs/[id]) classified with a deterministic web PORT so hand-added jobs also get a setting. Additive migration only; feature dormant until a profile sets setting_filter.

Activity-gated auto-fetch + new-jobs emails (commit 634f2a3, migration 079). Scheduled runs gated in worker gate.ts: warn email at 14d inactive, auto-pause at 30d or on dead subscription (canceled/unpaid/incomplete_expired, or trialing >24h past trial_end; MISSING subscriptions row = never paused — founder/beta safety). Manual runs never gated. Pause = is_active=false + BullMQ scheduler removed + profile_pause_state row; resume is explicit via banner on /dashboard/profiles, never automatic. New-jobs emails: auto runs only, 15-min sweep (notify-sweep global cron), 5-min settle window, one batched email per user, claim-based idempotency throughout. Unsubscribe = HMAC(JOBTRACKR_HMAC_SECRET)-signed link + notify_new_jobs toggle on My CV page. last_seen_at maintained by touch_user_engagement() SECURITY DEFINER RPC called fire-and-forget from dashboard layout (1h throttle in SQL).

operational_notes.title: Role vertical comes from My CV ONLY (contact_details.role_families); per-profile dropdown removed
operational_notes.detail: ROOT CAUSE of the Rashmi/Moran fabrication (tailored CV dropped real employers + regurgitated the composer prompt's aged-care worked example, with bracket placeholders like '[Placement Facility Name]'): the role vertical was being sourced from the WRONG field. The user picks role type ONCE in My CV ('What roles are you applying for?' → user_preferences.contact_details.role_families, 'applies to all CVs'), but all three analysis entry points read the SEPARATE per-search-profile field search_profiles.target_verticals. That field defaulted to 'general', and explicit-vertical routing (ac1b772) made it authoritative — _resolve_base_family mapped 'general'→'master', short-circuiting JD detection → generic master role pack (Projects section + 'Professional Experience' heading instead of the nursing pack) → composer dropped real roles. Provider-agnostic (reproduced deterministically in routing); NOT a gpt-vs-Claude issue. FIX (branch claude/cv-placeholder-parser-fix-8eivv7): (1) backend role_families.py _resolve_base_family — generic/catch-all hints ('general'/'other'/'master'/empty) no longer short-circuit to master; they fall through to JD detection, master only as final fallback. Specific hints (nursing/tech/manual + aliases) stay authoritative. Tests: tests/test_role_family_routing.py. (2) All three analysis entry points now source the vertical from My CV (contact_details.role_families[0]) with search_profiles.target_verticals as legacy fallback: analyze/route.ts, analyze/[run_id]/resume/route.ts, worker triggerAutoAnalyze.ts (worker also now PASSES target_vertical explicitly — was relying on backend JD auto-detect). Same source drives ATS thresholds. (3) Removed the per-search-profile Role type dropdown from ProfileForm.tsx (steps renumbered 1–5); createProfile writes target_verticals=[], updateProfile no longer overwrites it. KNOWN FOLLOW-UP (not done): dashboard board atsBand thresholds (dashboard/page.tsx, profiles/[id]/jobs/page.tsx) still compute from search_profiles.target_verticals for DISPLAY — should also read My CV for full consistency. DEPLOYS NEEDED: Vercel (frontend, on merge), flyctl deploy worker (auto-analyze), flyctl deploy cv-backend (role_families fallthrough). (4) STALE-CACHE SELF-HEAL: normalized_cv_text is only a rendered CACHE of structured_cv and can drift stale/empty while structured_cv is correct (verified live: Rashmi's structured_cv showed all 3 roles + 2 education entries, but the composer received a hollow normalized_cv_text and fabricated Experience/Education from the education_rules.py format template '### Institution Name | Location' → '[Institution Name]'/'[Location]', plus the section heading 'Experience' + home suburb as a phantom employer). FIX: when a parsed/verified structured_cv exists, analyze/route.ts AND worker triggerAutoAnalyze.ts now RE-RENDER normalized_cv_text from structured_cv at run time (cv-backend /internal/render-canonical-cv, pure no-AI) and self-heal the stored cache (best-effort UPDATE), falling back to the stored cache then raw cv_text on any render failure — never blocks analysis. After this, two consecutive manual runs on different jobs produced fully correct tailored CVs (real employers Uniting/Jesmond/Anglicare, Cert IV, BESTMed/MedMobile). Resume route uses the run's raw cv_text and is unaffected.

operational_notes.title: BACKLOG — adding a new vertical (e.g. accounting) is isolated + additive; recipe
operational_notes.detail: USER REQUEST (2026-06-25, deferred — NOT built): add new verticals one at a time (accounting next) WITHOUT touching the finalized nursing/tech/manual analysis pipelines. CONFIRMED SAFE: the per-vertical isolation work (feat/vertical-pipeline, merged to main 63463de) makes this possible. The analysis pipeline (steps, enforcers, ATS scoring, classifier) is DATA-DRIVEN off each vertical's RoleFamilyProfile read from the registry — it does NOT branch per vertical — so a new vertical cannot change how existing verticals are analysed (each reads its own PROFILE). The only vertical-specific code (nursing/hooks.py subtype detection; the `if rf.id == "nursing"` aged-care/registration branches) is gated to nursing and simply won't fire for a new vertical (hooks=None). RECIPE to add 'accounting' (all ADDITIVE — never edits existing verticals' data): BACKEND (1) new folder backend/api/app/services/verticals/accounting/ with config.py (PROFILE = RoleFamilyProfile(id='accounting', ...)), prompts.py (JD_ANALYSIS_HINTS), lexicon.json; (2) register in verticals/__init__.py: add the two imports + one VERTICALS['accounting'] = VerticalPack(...) entry (ROLE_FAMILIES + FAMILY_TO_LEXICON derive automatically); (3) verticals/__init__.py lexicon_path() has a HARDCODED folder_map (~line 100) — add 'accounting': 'accounting'. FRONTEND (only if it should be user-pickable): (4) lib/cv/skillLabels.ts — add 'accounting' to the RoleFamily union type + a LABELS entry; (5) the vertical-options arrays duplicated in ProfileDetailsClient.tsx (~line 249), ProfileSettingsClient.tsx, and ProfileForm.tsx. OPTIONAL DX IMPROVEMENT for the NEXT vertical: drive the frontend option lists + the lexicon_path folder_map from a single source so a new vertical becomes a pure drop-in (the only two non-additive friction points today). No DB/schema changes; no pipeline forking.

operational_notes.title: TWO tailored-CV prompts: composition.py is PRODUCTION, tailored_cv.py is LEGACY fallback
operational_notes.detail: The orchestrator (app/services/pipeline/orchestrator.py ~line 346) branches on get_settings().TAILORED_CV_WRITER == 'w8_verified'. Production sets the w8_verified Fly secret, so the LIVE path is run_tailored_cv_w8_verified → app/services/eval/writers/_impl.py _writer_w8_integrated → system prompt built by build_composition_system(role_family, seniority) from app/services/ai/prompts/variants/composition.py (the CAREER-STYLE SUMMARY block lives at composition.py ~lines 278-396). The OTHER prompt, app/services/ai/prompts/TAILORED_CV_SYSTEM in prompts/tailored_cv.py, is the LEGACY writer (run_tailored_cv in pipeline/steps/tailored_cv.py) used ONLY when the w8 flag is off. When changing tailored-CV / summary GENERATION behaviour that must show up in the UI, edit composition.py — editing tailored_cv.py alone has NO production effect. composition.py is the most behaviour-sensitive file in the repo (tuned by the real-test loop + golden-JD harness); change it deliberately and re-run the golden tests.

operational_notes.title: Career Highlights word-count: 3-way ceiling contradiction fixed (35-50 single source of truth)
operational_notes.detail: User reported summaries shipping under 25 words despite the composer prompt's '35-50 words HARD MINIMUM' rule. Root cause: the deterministic pipeline only had a MAX-word trimmer (_enforce_career_highlights_words in pipeline/steps/tailored_cv.py, default max_words=65) and an advisory-only validator gate (_gate_profile_word_count in pipeline/steps/tailored_structural_validation.py, pass-band 35-60/absolute-max 80) that the orchestrator logs but never acts on — nothing ever raised a too-SHORT summary back up. Fix has two parts: (1) added app/services/eval/writers/_impl.py:_ensure_career_highlights_floor(), a one-shot corrective retry wired into _writer_w8_integrated right after to_canonical() and before _enforce_structure() — if Career Highlights is below 35 words it asks the model to expand with CV-grounded facts only, accepting the retry only if it strictly improves on the original word count (falls back to original on any failure/non-improvement, never loops). (2) aligned all three previously-inconsistent numeric thresholds (50 prompt / 65 trimmer / 60-80 validator) to the same 35-50 target band: trimmer max_words default 65→50 (tailored_cv.py), validator pass-band 35-60→35-50 with absolute hard-fail moved 80→65 (tailored_structural_validation.py). Test tests/test_tailored_cv_step.py::test_enforce_career_highlights_default_cap_is_65 renamed/updated to assert 50. Also merged the years/seniority rule that was stated independently at composition.py ~285-288 (S1 framing), ~368-370 (seniority-adjective ban), and ~392-396 (SENIORITY WORDS) into one authoritative SENIORITY WORDS block; the other two locations now just point to it. Full suite: 960 passed after all edits.

operational_notes.title: Profile availability is an opt-in italic line under Registration & Licences, not a CV section
operational_notes.detail: Profile credentials JSON gained two fields (contact_details.credentials.availability: subset of ["Full Time","Part Time","Casual"]; show_availability: boolean). When show_availability is true AND >=1 type is ticked, build_availability_line (backend/api/app/services/cv/contact_line.py) yields 'Available: ...' text (canonical order via _AVAILABILITY_ORDER) which stamp_credentials renders as its OWN italic markdown line (*Available: ...*) under the ## Registration & Licences heading, separated from the licences line by a blank line. Deliberately NOT a new CV section, so the tailored-CV writer/composition.py is untouched (zero regression risk); availability is also excluded from build_credentials_line (the licences line stays clean). Off by default. Role-gated to nursing+manual. PDF italic via pdf_generator.py _md_inline *...*→<i>. UI: Availability control lives in the ACTIVE unified My CV component frontend/web/src/components/cv/ProfileDetailsClient.tsx CredentialsSection (shown when nursing||manual). NOTE: an earlier copy was added to ProfileSettingsClient.tsx, but the unified My CV page (commit dd0e74b) renders ProfileDetailsClient and only imports TYPES from ProfileSettingsClient — so the card had to be ported there (this was a real 'I don't see availability' gap). Sanitised/whitelisted in frontend/web/src/app/api/user/preferences/route.ts. JSON-only — no migration. Tests in test_credentials_stamp.py. Initial chip version shipped via PR #7 (squash-merged to main 2026-06-21); moved to its own italic line in a follow-up on main.

operational_notes.title: MULTI-ROLE summary anchor gap: deterministic net can't repair cherry-picked S2 — fixed with a generation-time retry
operational_notes.detail: Bug: when a CV has 2+ multi-month (nameable-anchor) employers, composition.py requires summary S2 to name BOTH (two semicolon clauses). The model sometimes names only one (e.g. the employer that gave an award) and drops the other. The deterministic net _enforce_company_anchor (pipeline/steps/tailored_cv.py:695) could NOT catch this: its 'already anchored' guard uses any(emp in prose for top2) — so a single named employer short-circuits it — and its regex append cannot restructure an award-shaped/partially-anchored S2 into two clauses (would double-name). Fix (per D21, honesty enforced at GENERATION time): added _ensure_summary_anchors_both_employers in app/services/eval/writers/_impl.py — a one-shot corrective retry wired into _writer_w8_integrated right AFTER _ensure_career_highlights_floor and BEFORE _enforce_structure. It reuses _extract_employers_from_cv; when 2+ anchors exist but <2 are named in the summary, it re-asks the model to rewrite S2 into two method+outcome clauses naming both (mixed tense, no tool names, no award-as-S2). Accepts the rewrite ONLY if it now names both top-2 employers AND stays exactly 2 sentences; otherwise keeps the original. Never loops, never fabricates. The deterministic _enforce_company_anchor still runs afterwards as a final no-op safety net. Tests: tests/test_summary_anchor_retry.py (5). Full suite 1160 green. NOTE: _ensure_summary_anchors_both_employers does NOT change the title-slot rule — opener must still equal the JD job title exactly (separate enforcer _enforce_summary_opener).

operational_notes.title: Credential prose-capture: trim now stops at orphan ')'/sentence period, not just '('
operational_notes.detail: Bug: JD credential extraction surfaced 'Cert III in Disability or Individual Support). Strong' — trailing prose leaked into the credential. Root cause in app/services/skills/post_process.py _trim_qual_phrase: the tail walk only broke on a token STARTING with '(' (the '(or equivalent)' case) plus a small prose stop-word whitelist. Malformed JD text with an ORPHAN closing paren (')' with no matching '(') and prose like 'Strong …' had nothing to break on, so it leaked. Fix: added _CRED_TAIL_TERMINATOR_RE = [).;:] — when a tail token contains a closing paren / sentence period / ';' / ':', keep the clean prefix before it, drop the rest, stop. Comma is deliberately NOT a terminator (real names like 'ageing, home and community'). Existing '(or' break + prose stop-words unchanged. Tests in tests/test_skills_post_process.py (test_trim_qual_phrase_*). Full suite 1162 green. NOTE: production cv-backend on Fly (jobtrackr-cv-api) must be redeployed (flyctl deploy) for the fix to affect live JD analyses; the rest of the displayed match output (60/60% Care/Soft, WWCC+NDIS missing, Cert IV⊒Cert III via AQF subsumption) was correct.

operational_notes.title: Teaser-rescue keyword filter was silently disabled (url_hash empty at stage 4b)
operational_notes.detail: Bug in backend/worker/src/pipeline/keywordFilter.ts applyKeywordFilter: the teaser-rescue path (active when profile.must_include_phrases is set) built its 'already passed by title' set from job.url_hash. But url_hash is only assigned later in the dedup stage (normalise() sets it to ''), so at stage 4b every job's url_hash is ''. Result: when ANY title matched, passedHashes={''} → titleRejected=jobs.filter(j=>!has(''))=[] → teaser rescue never ran, so jobs whose TITLE lacks the phrase but whose DESCRIPTION contains it (the whole point of smart-filter rescue) were dropped. Fix: key the passed-set on job.url (canonical URL, already populated by normalise at this stage) instead of url_hash. Verified via tsx repro: a 4-job set with 1 title-match + 2 desc-only matches went from 1 kept (buggy) to 3 kept (fixed). NOTE: only affects profiles using must_include_phrases. Diagnosing a specific 'no new jobs saved' report: each run already logs per-stage kept/dropped counts to run_logs.log_lines (visible in the LiveLogConsole) — 'stage 4b keyword filter: X kept, Y dropped' and 'stage 4c smart filter: ... title missing required / title excluded / desc excluded' pinpoint which gate (and which user rule) zeroes the run. Worker must be redeployed to Fly (flyctl deploy --config backend/worker/fly.toml) for the fix to take effect. CONFIRMED LIVE on profile 2bab2daa (AIN Sydney) via run_logs.log_lines: a 76-job run went stage 4b 76->13 (rescue bug dropped 63 genuine AIN jobs whose title wasn't the exact must_include phrase), then stage 4c 13->2 and stage 7b 2->0 via the user's 'Description must NOT contain' list (home care / Acute Care boilerplate). Rescue fix is the PRIMARY recovery (brings back the 63); desc-exclusion is a secondary trim the user can relax. ALSO ADDED (same commit set): per-phrase attribution on desc-exclusion (excludeByDescription now returns byPhrase; formatExcludeBreakdown renders '[home care: 8, acute care: 3]') logged at stage 4c and 7b, plus 100%-drop tripwire WARNs at stages 4b/4c/7b so a future all-dropped run names the offending rule in the log instantly.

operational_notes.title: Aurora — signature dual theme (dark hero + light twin) + density control
operational_notes.detail: New, original design language added as TWO themes: 'aurora-dark' (default) + 'aurora-light'. 100% additive + scoped — existing 5 themes byte-for-byte unchanged, pipeline/analysis/workflow untouched (CSS + theme registry + fonts only). Signature = teal (--brand) + violet (--violet) 'aurora' gradient, Space Grotesk display headings, Plus Jakarta Sans body, JetBrains Mono numerals, layered elevation, ambient canvas glow. All token pairs WCAG-AA verified (ratios documented in globals.css). (2) lib/themes.ts — registered both (Theme type, THEMES with previews, VALID_IDS, THEMED_CLASSES); getStoredTheme default flipped 'notion'->'aurora-dark'. (3) layout.tsx — next/font Plus_Jakarta_Sans/Space_Grotesk/JetBrains_Mono (--font-jakarta/-grotesk/-jbmono; jakarta+grotesk preload, mono lazy; manrope/noto flipped to preload:false as no longer default); FOUC default 'notion'->'aurora-dark' + regex incl aurora-* + density attr. (4) NEW lib/density.ts + components/DensityPickerClient.tsx ('Text size' Compact/Comfortable/Spacious, localStorage 'jobtrackr-density', FOUC-applied). (5) settings/theme/page.tsx — added density picker. Default density 'comfortable' = --ui-scale 1 = no change for other themes. Public pages (landing/auth) unaffected — full-bleed with own palettes. Charts (PipelineDonut canvas, PipelineFunnel) auto-adapt via tokens; their fixed slice hexes already read on dark. Verified: tsc clean + next build exit 0 (fonts resolve, Tailwind compiles). Standalone aurora-preview.html exists at repo root (delivered to user, NOT committed). Known minor: a few callout chips use hardcoded Tailwind light palettes (bg-amber-50 etc.) — readable on dark but not token-themed; future token sweep optional.

operational_notes.title: Credential trim — drop trailing prose adjectives + dangling connectors ('Bachelor of Nursing and ready')
operational_notes.detail: Recurring credential prose-capture (follows OPS-24). JD credential extraction surfaced 'Bachelor of Nursing and ready' (and other run-to-run tails) instead of 'Bachelor of Nursing'. _trim_qual_phrase (skills/post_process.py) walked the tail after _QUAL_PATTERN and only stopped on a small prose set + paren/period terminators — 'and'/'ready' passed through. Fix: (1) expanded _CRED_PROSE_TAIL_STOP with prose adjectives/verbs (ready, able, available, eager, keen, enthusiastic, willing, interested, ideally, preferred, desirable, essential, plus, advantageous, looking, seeking, wanting, equivalent, must, should); (2) added _CRED_TAIL_CONNECTORS and a post-walk loop that strips trailing connector/filler tokens (and/or/with/for/to/in/of/a/an/the/&/...) so we never leave a dangling 'Nursing and'. Legit '...and Community' continuations are preserved (the stop fires on the prose word, not on 'and' itself; the connector-strip only trims the LAST token). Verified: 'bachelor of nursing and ready[ to start]' → 'bachelor of nursing'; 'certificate iv in ageing, home and community care' unchanged; OPS-24 orphan-paren case unchanged. operational_notes.title: Employer anchor stamped-then-stripped by verify_claims — re-run anchor net post-verify
operational_notes.detail: Same stamp-then-strip class as OPS-31 (availability), now for the SUMMARY EMPLOYER ANCHOR. Symptom (Rashmi, post-deploy): S2 = 'Delivers safe, high-quality care, including electronic medication support for residents living with dementia.' — ZERO employers named despite the CV having Uniting + The Jesmond Group. Root cause: _enforce_company_anchor runs mid-pipeline inside _enforce_structure (_impl.py:654, pre-verify); verify_claims (line 1064) then rewrites the summary and drops the employer name(s), and the anchor net was NEVER re-applied afterward. Fix: re-run _enforce_company_anchor(verified_md, cv_text) in the post-verify chain (imported into _impl.py; placed just before the availability re-stamp). It is idempotent — no-op when both top-2 employers are already named, ZERO-named → append 'at E1 and E2.', PARTIAL → splice missing employer (OPS-30 logic). _find_summary_block matches 'Professional Summary' (the post-verify heading) so the re-run resolves the section correctly. Full suite 1166 passes. Deploy: flyctl deploy --config backend/api/fly.toml. NOTE the user's pasted example was from a deploy predating OPS-30/31/32 — this commit is the systemic fix that makes the anchor survive verify.

operational_notes.title: Availability line was stamped then stripped by verify_claims — re-stamp + verify-skip + idempotency
operational_notes.detail: Root cause of 'availability not appearing on tailored CV' (user-diagnosed, confirmed in code): stamp_availability_in_summary runs mid-pipeline inside _writer_w8_integrated (_impl.py:747), but the w8_verified entry point then calls verify_claims (_impl.py:1064) + a long post-verify re-pass chain that NEVER re-stamps availability. verify._collect_summary bundled the italic '*Available: …*' line into the summary prose (its bullet guard skips '* ' but the note starts '*A' with no space), so when the summary was repaired the line was deleted before storage — affecting BOTH the .md preview and the PDF (same stored file). THREE fixes: (1) verify.py _collect_summary now skips lines matching ^\*?\s*available: (regex) so the verifier never treats availability as a fact-claim. (2) _impl.py run_tailored_cv_w8_verified re-stamps availability LAST (after verify + all re-passes, before result.tailored_md=verified_md). (3) stamp_availability_in_summary made IDEMPOTENT — strips any pre-existing availability line (and the blank we inserted before it) before re-appending, so the double call never duplicates and produces stable output. Tests: test_availability_stamp_is_idempotent + existing summary tests. Full suite 1166 passes. Deploy: flyctl deploy --config backend/api/fly.toml. (UI copy fix 'Professional Summary' already shipped in OPS-30.)

operational_notes.title: Anchor enforcer — partial cherry-pick: splice missing employer + past-tense fix
operational_notes.detail: Symptom from a v370-deployed run (Rashmi): summary S2 = 'Provide person-centred care… at Uniting, and have served as a primary Medication Assistant.' — one employer (Uniting) named, second (The Jesmond Group) dropped. _enforce_company_anchor's original any()-guard treated this as 'already anchored' and skipped. Fix: enforcer now handles THREE cases — (a) BOTH named → no-op; (b) ZERO named → append 'at E1 and E2.' (legacy); (c) PARTIAL → splice the missing employer into a trailing ', and (have) verb-ed' tail (= the cherry-picked second-role clause) AND convert 'have served' (present-perfect) → 'served' (simple past) per the completed-role tense rule. When no such tail exists, append a fresh '; at <missing>.' clause. Module-level _DANGLING_END_RE moved out of the function. New regression tests in test_tailored_cv_step.py: test_enforce_company_anchor_partial_cherry_pick_splices_missing_employer (Rashmi's exact case) + test_enforce_company_anchor_partial_appends_when_no_tail. operational_notes.title: Upstash Redis billing fix: never use BullMQ job schedulers (10s block cap) + Fly zombie VM incident
operational_notes.detail: 2026-07-10: Upstash pay-per-command bill (~6M cmds/mo, $12) traced via live MONITOR to BullMQ idle polling — 1.03 cmds/sec, 100% bull:jobtrackr-pipeline:* keys. TWO fixes shipped: (1) Worker opts drainDelay 60s + stalledInterval 5min (6830556). (2) In-process cron timers (cron-parser, UTC-pinned) replace BullMQ job schedulers entirely (402968b) — CRITICAL: any delayed job in Redis caps the worker's blocking wait at a hardcoded 10s (bullmq worker.js maximumBlockTimeout=10), silently ignoring drainDelay, and a job scheduler keeps a delayed job there permanently. Timers enqueue normal queue jobs at tick time; retry opts and manual-run path unchanged; tick landing mid-restart is skipped. Legacy scheduler state (9 delayed jobs incl. 6 for deleted profiles + 115 repeat:* keys) purged one-time directly in Redis (getJobSchedulers() did NOT see them — old-format entries). Measured after: 0.147 cmds/sec idle (~12.7k/day, ~0.38M/mo observed), 7x cut. REMAINING (user console action): remove read regions from the 'jobtrackr' global Upstash DB — each write is billed once per replica region (~50% of BullMQ idle commands are writes; explains observed-vs-billed ~2x gap). ALSO: Fly zombie VM incident — the 09:03Z deploy left the OLD VM running and polling Redis while the control plane said 'stopped' (new VM failed TapOpen ResourceBusy, then 'lack of capacity' in syd); fly ssh/exec refuse in this state. Fix: fly machine destroy --force (zombie's Redis connection died ~45s later) + fresh deploy. Watch for recurrence after deploys: fly machine list state vs fly logs activity. Also: REDIS_URL lives ONLY in Fly secrets (Vercel copies are Encrypted/sensitive); extractable via one-off machine: fly machine run alpine sleep 600 -a jobtrackr-worker + fly machine exec.

operational_notes.title: Automated zombie-VM guard: heartbeat keys + verify-worker CI job (BullMQ getWorkersCount unreliable on Upstash)
operational_notes.detail: 2026-07-11: automated detection of the OPS-36 zombie-VM pattern. Tried BullMQ's built-in getWorkersCount()/getWorkers() (CLIENT LIST-based) first — REJECTED: two independent fresh connections to the 'jobtrackr' Upstash DB both reported 0 workers while the real worker was demonstrably alive and ticking on its 15-min notify-sweep schedule (confirmed via fly logs). Upstash's CLIENT LIST does not reliably reflect connections from other client instances (proxy/vendor quirk) — do NOT use CLIENT LIST-based checks against this Upstash instance for anything correctness-sensitive. FIX: application-level heartbeat instead (backend/worker/src/queue/heartbeat.ts) — each worker writes worker:heartbeat:<FLY_MACHINE_ID> (EX 150s, refreshed every 60s via setInterval, cleared in index.ts shutdown()). New CI job 'verify-worker' in deploy.yml (needs: deploy-worker) runs checkDuplicateWorkers.ts: SCANs worker:heartbeat:*, polls up to 60s (grace window for a normal rolling-deploy overlap), FAILS the workflow (red X + GH notification, chosen 'alert only' over unattended auto-destroy) if >1 heartbeat persists, printing the stale key(s) plus the exact fly machine destroy remediation. New repo secret REDIS_URL_WORKER (GitHub Actions) added via gh secret set — value extracted through the same throwaway-Fly-machine trick as before, never written to a durable file. VALIDATED end-to-end: (1) real push through the full pipeline — verify-worker passed in 19s, correctly saw exactly 1 heartbeat (784595ea63ee98); (2) planted a fake second heartbeat key locally and ran the real check script against production Redis — correctly detected 2, retried through all 6 attempts, failed loudly with remediation text, exit code 1; (3) deleted the fake key, re-ran — passed immediately. 65 tests (5 new, heartbeat.test.ts).

deferred_features.detail:
Goal: one global 30-day bucket every user reads first; a fetch scrapes only the delta of days not yet covered for its search slice (keyword x location-cell x source). Filter-on-read so one user's exclusions never starve another (home-care vs aged-care). Tier rule (existing): SEEK full JD shared with all; Adzuna full JD shown only to unlimited even when cached; weekly/monthly see Adzuna snippet. 30-day retention; eviction keeps rows referenced by applications/analysis.

PHASE A done this session (additive, safe, flag-free because inert):
  - migration 066_search_coverage.sql (NOT yet applied in Supabase — manual SQL editor step).
  - backend/worker/src/pipeline/coverage.ts (resolveSlices/normaliseKeyword/recordCoverage write-only; readCoverage/computeDeltaDays defined for Phase B but NOT called).
  - orchestrator.ts wires recordCoverage() after a successful run, try/catch-guarded (missing table no-ops). Worker typecheck passes. No behavior change to existing scrape depth.

KEY DECOMPOSITION DECISION: acting on coverage (scrape-delta + bucket serve) is COUPLED to the global_jobs/profile_jobs split — making B's run scrape only the delta without serving it the rows A already has would REGRESS B's results. So lookback-flip + serve ship together in Phase B, not before. Phase A only warms the ledger.

PHASE B (next, needs gates): migration-checker first; global_jobs (bucket, snippet+full JD + jd_access) + profile_jobs (RLS per-user state); backfill jobs; dual-write; reads behind flag; eviction cron. OPEN DECISION #1: keep analysis_runs.job_id on jobs vs repoint to profile_jobs (shapes backfill/cutover) — settle before B.
PHASE C: single-flight slice lock (refreshing/refresh_started_at) + Adzuna upgrade-on-read.
PHASE D: admin observability + cutover. NEW OPEN ITEM: bucket-served runs vs the live weekly=30/monthly=120 run caps (enforced web-layer only today) — decide whether a 0-scrape bucket read counts against the cap.

VERIFICATION REALITY: worker has no test harness (tsc --noEmit only); validation is the Vercel-preview gate per phase.

PHASE B FOUNDATION done this session (dormant, flag-guarded, pushed to main):
  - migration 067_global_jobs.sql (canonical bucket: url_hash unique, snippet+full JD + jd_access, matched_keywords gin, lat/lng null until geocode-on-write, service-role RLS). NOT applied.
  - migration 068_profile_jobs.sql (per-user link+state, RLS via search_profiles mirroring jobs). NOT applied.
  - backend/worker/src/pipeline/bucket.ts: dualWriteBucket() behind USE_GLOBAL_BUCKET env flag (default off). Best-effort try/catch (missing table no-ops). Merges matched_keywords across runs. deriveJdAccess: seek/careerjet/board='all', adzuna direct='unlimited_only', adzuna api='snippet'.
  - orchestrator.ts calls dualWriteBucket after saveJobs. jobs stays source of truth; nothing reads bucket yet.
  - Worker typecheck passes. With flag OFF, save path is byte-for-byte unchanged -> zero production impact.

STILL DORMANT/NOT DONE (need preview gate + decisions, NOT pushed):
  - Backfill migration (069) collapsing existing jobs into global_jobs/profile_jobs (one-time, over prod data).
  - Web read-path switch (dashboard/applications/analyze reading profile_jobs behind a flag) — the production-affecting part; deliberately NOT done blind.
  - Coverage-driven lookback flip (couples with serve; ship with read switch).
  - Phase C single-flight lock + Adzuna upgrade-on-read. Phase D observability + analysis_runs FK repoint (decision #1) + cutover.
  - Precise snippet-vs-full JD split (foundation approximates description as both; refine at read build).

TO ACTIVATE B: apply 067+068 in Supabase SQL editor, set worker env USE_GLOBAL_BUCKET=true (fly secrets), run a profile, verify global_jobs/profile_jobs populate. Still no UI change until read path is built.

=== ARCHITECTURE PIVOT 2026-06-25: serve-into-jobs ===
31 web sites read jobs + analysis_runs.job_id FKs jobs.id -> rejected switching reads to profile_jobs (too large/risky). CHOSEN: worker serves the bucket INTO jobs. global_jobs(067)+search_coverage(066) are the shared bucket+ledger; worker scrapes coverage-driven delta, upserts to global_jobs, then materialises each profile's jobs rows FROM the bucket (tier-projected JD + profile filters + per-user distance). RESULT: zero web changes, NO analysis_runs FK repoint (decision #1 MOOT), profile_jobs(068) reserved/unused. All gated by USE_GLOBAL_BUCKET (default off).

BUILT on feat/global-bucket (typecheck clean, NOT merged, NOT deployed, migrations NOT applied):
  - bucket.ts: bucketEnabled flag; upsertGlobalJobs (canonical upsert + matched_keywords merge + deriveJdAccess seek/careerjet/board=all, adzuna-direct=unlimited_only, adzuna-api=snippet); serveProfileFromBucket (select by location_cell+30d window -> map -> tier JD projection (adzuna full gated to unlimited) -> applyKeywordFilter+postFetchFilter+working-rights -> per-user distanceFor -> NormalisedJob[] for saveJobs); evictStaleBucket (DELETE first_seen_at<30d, safe because global_jobs referenced by nothing).
  - coverage.ts: sliceDeltaDays (cold=30d, fresh<6h=0, else days+1), computeProfileLookback (max delta across slices, allFresh), acquireSliceLocks/releaseSliceLocks (atomic single-flight, BUILT but NOT wired -- herd protection currently via TTL-driven lookback=0).
  - orchestrator.ts: bucket-mode lookback (coverage-driven) replaces per-profile run_logs lookback; after stage 11b -> upsertGlobalJobs(scraped) then serveProfileFromBucket replaces toSave; evictStaleBucket at run start. Flag off = unchanged.
  - scripts/backfillBucket.ts: one-off warm-start seeding global_jobs from existing jobs (reuses upsertGlobalJobs for correct location_cell). Run: USE_GLOBAL_BUCKET=true tsx backfillBucket.

STILL OPEN / NOT DONE:
  - Phase C single-flight NOT wired (TTL covers most herd cases); Adzuna upgrade-on-read for unlimited readers of snippet-only cached rows = TODO (they see snippet until next scrape).
  - Phase D admin observability (bucket hit-rate / scrapes avoided / cost saved); bucket-served-run vs live run caps (weekly=30/monthly=120) policy decision.
  - matched_keywords used only as merge field; serve selects by location_cell+window (filters do precise gating) -- matched_keywords gin prefilter is an optimization not yet used.
  - geocode-on-write (store lat/lng on global_jobs) deferred; distance computed per-serve via distanceFor(location string) which is cached.

TO VALIDATE (preview gate): merge or check out branch; apply 066+067 in Supabase; set worker USE_GLOBAL_BUCKET=true; optionally run backfillBucket; run two profiles same keyword+location and confirm 2nd scrapes ~0 days yet sees full window; weekly sees adzuna snippet, unlimited sees full.

GEOCODE-ON-WRITE (2026-06-25, branch): global_jobs.lat/lng geocoded ONCE per posting in upsertGlobalJobs (reuses stored coords on repeat); serveProfileFromBucket computes per-user distance via new distanceFromCoords() (OSRM only, NO Nominatim 1.1s-gap geocode); orchestrator skips the stage-11b distance loop when bucketEnabled. Fixes the first-run distance bottleneck (~100 jobs x Nominatim = 650s) for any posting already bucketed. First scrape of a new posting still pays one-time Nominatim geocode.

=== HARDENING 2026-06-25 (all on main, deployed) ===
A. SINGLE-FLIGHT + SKIP-WHEN-FRESH (c4692ed): all-fresh slices skip stage 2 entirely (0 scrape); stale slices -> first caller claims per-slice refresh lock + scrapes, others skip+serve; cold slices (no coverage row) ALWAYS scrape (never skipped). Locks released post-run; 10-min stale guard for crashes. Validated earlier: lookback floor fix (0c2b7ba) + serve distance-bound (407cae7) + geocode region-bias (3ec4659) + geocode-on-write (38aa9d2).
C. OBSERVABILITY (614fba5): admin Sourcing page Global-bucket card (size, slices+fresh, scrapes avoided = completed runs fetched 0 saved >0, hit rate, per-source). Best-effort queries.
RUN-CAP DECISION: no change. Every pipeline run counts vs weekly-30/monthly-120 regardless of scrape amount; bucket makes runs cheaper not free; AI cost gated separately by CV/letter caps.
DEFERRED (by design): upgrade-on-read (Adzuna full-JD for unlimited readers of snippet-only rows) — zero effect until non-unlimited users populate snippet rows; adds Apify cost per serve; untestable now. Wire when first weekly/monthly user onboards. Also still: drop unused profile_jobs table; fold non-date sources; validate a real weekly-tier run shows Adzuna snippet.
